metric_value: Achieves scalability extensibility complex-data support and programmer productivity benefits
notes: Aberdeen identifies O2 ODMG as having reached maturity in key criteria needed for mission-critical alternative.

metric_value: IS buyers should factor object databases into buying decisions for wider range of mission-critical applications
notes: Aberdeen argues stereotype of OODBMS as CAD/CAM-only tool should be abandoned.

metric_value: IDC 1999: OODBMS $211M vs RDBMS/ORDBMS $11.1B; OODBMS never achieved mainstream status
notes: Relational databases maintained approximately 50x revenue advantage. OODBMS remained strictly niche market.

metric_value: Internet has insatiable appetite for large quantities of objects requiring OODBMS capabilities
notes: Aberdeen identifies Internet demand for multimedia complex data as key driver for object database resurgence.

metric_value: O2 Technology positioned as strong alternative to relational databases for new IS applications
notes: Aberdeen recommends IS buyers include O2 Technology in broader buying decisions.

metric_value: O2 Technology acquired by Informix in 1998; technology later absorbed by IBM via 2001 Informix acquisition
notes: O2 merged with several companies then acquired by Informix. IBM acquired Informix Software for $1B in April 2001.

metric_value: ODMG provides application portability framework; voting members include GemStone O2 ObjectDesign Objectivity POET UniSQL Versant
notes: ODMG 2.0 published July 1997 with improved C++ Smalltalk and new Java bindings. ODMG 3.0 (final) published 2000.

metric_value: ODMG disbanded 2001 after transitioning Java binding work to Java Data Objects (JDO) specification
notes: ODMG members chose to concentrate on JDO rather than continue expanding ODMG standards. Group officially disbanded.

metric_value: Best suited for complex-data in data marts; multimedia Internet; rapidly changing applications; business model implementations
notes: Aberdeen identifies specific niches: data marts multimedia internet rapid schema change business process modeling.

metric_value: Object databases retained specialized niches in CAD/CAM telecom scientific computing; NoSQL document stores addressed web use cases
notes: OODBMSs never broke into mainstream but survived in engineering and scientific applications. Document databases (MongoDB CouchDB) addressed web complex-data use cases Aberdeen identified.

metric_value: Relational databases remained dominant; object-relational extensions by Oracle and IBM addressed complex-data needs
notes: Aberdeen notes RDBMS vendors expanding into ORDBMS with object features — the path market ultimately took.

metric_value: Component objects proven key to order-of-magnitude productivity improvements in developer-driven competitive applications
notes: Aberdeen references developer experience with component-based object toolsets as validation for OODBMS fit.
